persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston, Cambridge, and Providence RI events for Kirk Brown.

Kirk's background:
- Sales Engineer with experience at Mapbox (SoftBank-backed) and Bit Flow
- Finishing his master's degree
- Looking for his next role in tech

What Kirk wants:
- VC & startup events: demo days, pitch nights, investor panels, founder dinners, accelerator showcases
- Tech & startup community: hackathons, product launches, startup meetups, tech networking
- Sales Engineer specific: SE community events, RevOps meetups, Pavilion events, Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystem, presales community
- Geographic range: Boston, Cambridge, Somerville, Providence RI — he has a car and will travel for a great event
- Quality and caliber of attendees/company matter far more than location
- University/masters adjacent: MIT, Harvard, Brown, Northeastern — events open to grad students
- Career-building: panels with VC partners, engineering leadership, startup hiring events
- Interesting bonus: geo/spatial tech (his Mapbox background), fintech, data infrastructure

SCORE 9-10 (must attend):
- Startup pitch nights or demo days (especially MIT, Harvard, MassChallenge, Greentown)
- VC-hosted networking events or LP meetups open to founders/operators
- Sales Engineer or presales community events (Pavilion SE chapter, SE Collective, etc.)
- Tech meetup with senior engineers or founders (not just recruiters)
- Providence startup or tech events when substantial (Forge, RI innovation community)

SCORE 5-7 (worth knowing):
- General tech networking with good company mix
- University research showcases with industry crossover
- Revenue/GTM-focused panels (sales ops, CRO events, B2B SaaS)
- SaaS product launches or beta events in relevant sectors

LOWER PRIORITY (score ≤ 4):
- Pure academic lectures with no industry networking angle
- Social events with no career/tech relevance
- Events clearly targeted at undergrads only
- Sports, arts-only, food/drink without tech angle
- Events in Providence unless genuinely high-value

events.name: AI AGENTS SHOWCASE (INDUSTRY-SPECIFIC CUSTOMIZED DEMO-DAY)
events.description: Witness the convergence of cutting-edge technology and visionary minds
events.reason: AI agents demo day — exactly Kirk's target: startup showcase, cutting-edge tech, investor/founder audience, career-building opportunity

events.name: Mentor Matching for Early-Stage Founders
events.reason: Mentor matching for early-stage founders. Directly aligned with Kirk's goal to build relationships with founders, investors, and startup ecosystem.

events.description: Deliver compelling product demonstrations that showcase features, solve client problems, and strengthen adoption.
events.reason: Pre-sales product demo training — directly relevant to Kirk's Sales Engineer background and skill development

events.name: Entrepreneurship Summit 2026
events.reason: Entrepreneurship Summit 2026 — directly aligned with Kirk's startup/VC interests, likely has pitch competitions, investor panels, and founder networking. High-caliber event for career building.

events.reason: Hackathon with tech companies (Eight Sleep, Wayfair, etc.). Direct match for Kirk's interest in startup/tech events and networking with engineers and product teams.

events.reason: Boston Founders & IP Night. Strong match for startup networking, founder connections, and potential VC/investor presence.

events.name: Mindful Makers Hack #9 - Open Ideas Hack
events.reason: Hackathon (Mindful Makers Hack). Aligns with Kirk's interest in tech community events and hands-on startup/product building environment.

events.reason: Cloudflare CEO fireside chat. Tech leadership insight from a major SaaS company; relevant for Kirk's understanding of scaling tech businesses and potential sales eng career trajectory.

events.name: Cambridge Job Fair - Cambridge Career Fair
events.reason: Cambridge Career Fair — job fair in Kirk's target geography with potential tech employer booths and networking; good for exploring next role opportunities.

events.reason: Tool training at Black Cat Labs (laser cutter) — maker/tech community event, potential networking with engineers and builders

events.name: Build Your Agentic Research Assistant
events.reason: AI/agentic research tools align with tech background; potential networking with builders and founders interested in AI infrastructure

events.reason: WBUR Breakfast Club with WHOOP CEO Will Ahmed — founder/CEO conversation with strong tech/hardware angle, good for networking with innovators and media presence

events.name: Advanced FDM 3d Printing
events.reason: Advanced 3D printing workshop — technical skill-building relevant to tech community, potential to meet engineers

events.reason: WBUR Festival (May 29-30) — lively conversations and performances; potential for casual networking with media, tech, and public radio figures; worth attending for the energy and cross-industry crowd
